prompt: Deserializing an old saved file throws InvalidClassException: local class incompatible. What is the bug?
choices: Adding a field always causes a NullPointerException, not an InvalidClassException
Without a declared serialVersionUID, Java auto-generates one from the class structure — changing the class changes the UID, making old files incompatible
Serializable classes cannot add fields after the first serialization
The new timeout field must also be transient for deserialization to succeed
explanation: When serialVersionUID is not declared, Java computes it from the class structure (fields, methods, etc.). Adding a field changes the structure, which changes the computed UID. Old saved data has the old UID; the new class has a new UID — they don't match, so InvalidClassException is thrown. Fix: declare private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L; in both versions to maintain compatibility. New fields will be initialized to their default values when loading old data.
